verdicts.reasoning: The description of Slack as a work operating system that enhances communication and collaboration is supported by the homepage content.
The evidence confirms that Slack uses OAuth 2.0 and tokens as authentication methods, aligning with the recorded value.
The evidence supports that developers can create Slack Apps and obtain credentials without human approval, confirming the recorded value.
The evidence confirms that Slack provides a broad Web API and additional APIs, supporting the recorded value.
The existence of public documentation and self-serve credential creation supports the claim that integrations can be built immediately.
